facts.text: Weston's name changed from Indian Trace in the early 1980s, and 1984 saw the first homes built in the Arvida‑developed Country Isles and Windmill Ranch.
In 1996, Weston voters chose to incorporate as a city, keeping local tax revenue in town rather than being annexed by neighboring cities.
The Weston Branch Library, part of the Broward County Library system, sits on Bonaventure Boulevard and serves the community’s reading needs.
Peace Mound Park, a tranquil spot with historic roots, offers serene walking paths around a lake that once marked early settlement.
Weston experiences a tropical monsoon climate, staying humid and sunny for most of the year with dry, warm winters during La Niña.
Major companies such as Marriott International, American Express, and Flex Seal have large operations in Weston, reflecting its business-friendly environment.
